response.justification: The candidate has a broad engineering background spanning web, mobile, embedded, and systems programming, but their C++ experience is limited to an older role writing components for NI LabWindows and a bachelor thesis project, with C++ conspicuously absent from their current skills list. The overwhelming majority of their career is built on JavaScript/TypeScript/Node.js with no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 security standards, MQTT, multi-threaded C++ system design, or industrial protocols central to this role.
strengths: Event-driven and micro-services architecture experience handling high-throughput workloads (millions of requests/second)
Demonstrated range across embedded, mobile, and full-stack domains including robotic arm development with D/Java
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source contributions, and active community knowledge-sharing
concerns: C++ is absent from the current skills list and last used substantively in a historically early role; no demonstrated modern C++/STL or concurrent systems expertise
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, or any domain-specific protocol required by this role
Primary technology stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React) is fundamentally misaligned with the C++-centric industrial platform engineering this position demands
